title: coronilla minima f. major
intro: Coronilla minima f. major, also known as the small crownwort or dwarf coronilla, is a perennial herb belonging to the Fabaceae family. It is a creeping plant with small yellow flowers, traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, and is used to alleviate conditions such as digestive problems, fever, and respiratory issues. Coronilla minima f. major can be prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in traditional folk medicine to treat symptoms of colds, coughs, and sore throats.
